trans-unit.source: Here you design your site's Bootstrap theme – without writing SCSS by hand. Pick colors, fonts, spacing and component styles in the editor, see the result instantly in the preview and publish it with one click.

You can start from one of the bundled Bootswatch presets and adapt it to your needs, or build your theme entirely yourself. The SCSS compilation is handled in the background by EXT:t3sbootstrap – you don't have to worry about it.
Select the site whose theme you want to edit below.
Adjust colors and variables in the editor – with Preview you can test safely, without changing the frontend.
With Publish you take your theme live, and with Export SCSS you download it as a ZIP.

While you use the Bootstrap Builder, you should no longer edit the SCSS fields directly in EXT:t3sbootstrap (T3SB Config). The builder is the single source for these values and fully overwrites the SCSS files on every publish. The option to edit this data in EXT:t3sbootstrap is therefore disabled – use the fields here in the builder instead.
Choose a site to design its Bootstrap theme:

Tip: Your changes are saved and pre-filled again when you reopen. A backup is created automatically before every publish – you'll find it in typo3conf/ext/t3sb_package/Resources/Public/T3SB-SCSS/ as file _<timestamp>-custom-variables-<id>.scss.
Pick a Bootswatch preset below as a starting point and adapt it to your needs – or build your theme entirely yourself. With Preview you test changes safely, with Publish you take them live.
Changing the preset pre-fills the fields below. Your own changes stay on top.
Click “Preview” or “Publish” once. The preview shows your freshly compiled theme CSS.
The frontend URL could not be determined for this site.
Really publish the theme? The changes will go live on the frontend.
Preview updated. The frontend is unchanged.
Published to frontend and compiled. Preview updated.
No t3sbootstrap config record found on the root page. Please create one first.
Theme saved, but no CSS was compiled. Ensure "customScss" is enabled in EXT:t3sbootstrap (CDN disabled) and the Bootstrap SCSS sources exist. The CSS will also be built on the next frontend request.
